misconceptionSummary: Students divide the divisor by each digit rather than each digit by the divisor.

lines.text: Divided the divisor BY the digits — backwards

steps.narration: The student divided three by each digit instead of each digit by three. This reverses the operation entirely — three divided by six is not the same as six divided by three.

visuals.ruleText: Always divide the dividend digit by the divisor — never the other way
visuals.subText: In 693 ÷ 3: the divisor is 3, so divide EACH digit of 693 by 3

steps.narration: The divisor is the number you are dividing by — it goes outside the bus-stop bracket. Every digit inside the bracket is divided by that outside number, not the reverse.
Six divided by three is two, nine divided by three is three, three divided by three is one. Each digit of the dividend is divided by the divisor — the answer is two hundred and thirty-one.

visuals.label: Dividend digits ÷ divisor — the divisor is always the number you divide BY

steps.narration: Two hundred and thirty-one. The divisor is always what you divide by — each digit inside the bracket is divided by the number outside, never the reverse.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: 693 ÷ 3 computed as 3÷6, 3÷9, 3÷3. The divisor 3 goes outside — divide each digit of 693 by 3, not the other way round.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: The divisor is what you divide BY. In 693 ÷ 3, divide each digit of 693 by 3 — not 3 by each digit. Direction matters.
